title: Warden of Form
description: The final Warden holds the boundary between everything that can be represented and everything that cannot. To pass, break what cannot be broken.

lines: There was no room. There was no corridor. There was no path that led here because here was not a place that had a path leading to it.
And yet the Warden of Form was here. Had always been here. Would always be here because here was what it was made of.
It did not have a body so much as it had a shape. The shape said: boundary. The shape said: this is where the representable ends.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I am Form. The Master's final shape. Everything before me had a shape I gave it.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: Your world had walls because I gave walls their angles. Your spells had shapes because I gave shapes their meaning. Your wounds had patterns because I gave damage its geometry.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: You built the rules of how everything looks.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I am the rules of how everything is. Looking is only one application.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: To pass, you must break what cannot be broken. This is not a riddle. It is a genuine requirement. I do not know if it can be done. I have been waiting here since before the question was asked.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: What does breaking Form look like?

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: I don't know. I have never seen it. If I had seen it, it would have had a shape. It would have been Form. Whatever breaks me must be something I have not yet given a shape to.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: So you've been guarding something you can't define how to get past.

lines.speaker: Warden of Form
lines.text: Yes. I find it clarifying. Most problems, when you strip away their shape, are simply the question: what are you willing to do? Demonstrate.
